en: The cycle of sending a request to Claude, checking stop_reason, executing requested tools, appending results to history, and iterating until end_turn.
sr: Ciklus: pošalji zahtev Claude-u, proveri stop_reason, izvrši tražene alate, dodaj rezultate u istoriju, ponavljaj dok ne dođe end_turn.

en: A structured field in the Claude API response indicating why the model stopped generating. Key values: 'tool_use' (model wants to call a tool) and 'end_turn' (model finished its turn).
sr: Strukturirano polje u odgovoru Claude API-ja koje govori zašto je model stao. Važne vrednosti: 'tool_use' (model hoće da pozove alat) i 'end_turn' (model je završio).

en: A stop_reason value (and content block type) signaling the model is requesting a tool call. The loop should continue: execute the tool and return its result. A tool_use block is also the right way to signal explicit TASK completion - have the model call a dedicated completion tool rather than relying on end_turn or natural-language parsing.
sr: Vrednost stop_reason-a (i tip bloka) koja znači da model traži poziv alata. Petlja se nastavlja: izvrši alat i vrati rezultat. tool_use blok je i pravi način da se signalizira eksplicitan ZAVRŠETAK ZADATKA - model pozove namenski completion tool umesto oslanjanja na end_turn ili parsiranje prirodnog jezika.

en: A stop_reason value signaling the model has finished its TURN (no more tool calls this turn) - the correct signal for terminating a single loop turn. IMPORTANT: end_turn means the turn ended, NOT that a higher-level TASK is complete; the model may end a turn to await tool results or user input. For explicit task-completion, use a dedicated completion tool, not end_turn.
sr: Vrednost stop_reason-a koja znači da je model završio svoj POTEZ (nema više poziva alata u tom potezu) - pravi signal za zaustavljanje jednog kruga petlje. VAŽNO: end_turn znači da je potez završen, NE da je viši ZADATAK gotov; model može završiti potez da sačeka rezultate alata ili korisnikov unos. Za eksplicitan završetak zadatka koristi namenski completion tool, ne end_turn.

en: A hook that intercepts an outgoing tool call BEFORE it executes. Used to enforce compliance deterministically (block, modify, or gate the call). Sometimes called PreToolUse.
sr: Hook koji presreće odlazeći poziv alata PRE nego što se izvrši. Koristi se za determinističko sprovođenje pravila (blokiraj, izmeni ili uslovi poziv). Ponekad se zove PreToolUse.

en: A hook that intercepts a tool RESULT after execution, used to transform/normalize data before the model processes it. Runs AFTER the action, so it cannot prevent it.
sr: Hook koji presreće REZULTAT alata posle izvršenja, za transformaciju/normalizaciju podataka pre nego što ih model obradi. Radi POSLE akcije, pa je ne može sprečiti.

en: Executing an operation in a way that reveals what would happen (e.g., EXPLAIN, or a rolled-back transaction) without making permanent changes.
sr: Izvršavanje operacije tako da se vidi šta bi se desilo (npr. EXPLAIN ili transakcija sa rollback-om) bez trajnih izmena.

en: The scope of impact an operation would have (e.g., how many rows a DELETE would affect). A dry run estimates blast radius before real execution.
sr: Obim uticaja koji bi operacija imala (npr. koliko redova bi DELETE pogodio). Dry run procenjuje blast radius pre stvarnog izvršenja.

en: Persistent data within a session, e.g., an approval token recording that a human approved an action. A hook can check it before allowing a gated operation.
sr: Trajni podaci unutar sesije, npr. approval token koji beleži da je čovek odobrio akciju. Hook može da ga proveri pre nego što dozvoli uslovljenu operaciju.

en: Guaranteeing a rule holds 100% of the time via code (hooks, prerequisite gates) rather than prompt instructions, which have a non-zero failure rate.
sr: Garantovanje da pravilo važi 100% vremena pomoću koda (hooks, prerequisite gates), a ne prompt instrukcija koje imaju nenulti procenat greške.

en: Breaking a complex workflow into smaller steps. The decomposition strategy should match the task's nature: predictable tasks use a fixed pipeline, open-ended tasks use dynamic adaptation.
sr: Rastavljanje složenog posla na manje korake. Strategija dekompozicije treba da odgovara prirodi zadatka: predvidljiv posao -> fiksni pipeline, otvoren posao -> dinamička adaptacija.

en: A decomposition pattern with predefined steps executed in the same order every time (prompt chaining). Deterministic, efficient, and easy to test - best for structured, predictable tasks.
sr: Obrazac dekompozicije sa unapred definisanim koracima koji se izvršavaju uvek istim redom (prompt chaining). Deterministički, efikasan, lak za testiranje - najbolji za strukturiran, predvidljiv posao. Anti-pattern kad se SVAKI upit (i najprostiji) gura kroz sve korake - tada treba dinamičko rutiranje.

en: A decomposition pattern where the agent decides at runtime which steps to take, how many, and in what order, based on what it discovers. Flexible but costlier and less predictable - best for open-ended, complex tasks.
sr: Obrazac dekompozicije gde agent u toku rada sam odlučuje koje korake da preduzme, koliko njih i kojim redom, na osnovu onoga što otkrije. Fleksibilan ali skuplji i manje predvidljiv - najbolji za otvoren, složen posao.

en: An API request parameter that controls whether and which tool the model must call. Values: 'auto' (model decides), 'any' (must call some tool), 'tool' with a name (must call that specific tool), 'none' (no tools). Forces a tool_use block - deterministic API-level enforcement, unlike a prompt.
sr: Parametar API zahteva koji kontroliše da li i koji alat model mora da pozove. Vrednosti: 'auto' (model bira), 'any' (mora neki alat), 'tool' sa imenom (mora baš taj alat), 'none' (bez alata). Iznuđuje tool_use blok - determinističko sprovođenje na nivou API-ja, za razliku od prompta.

en: A sampling parameter controlling the randomness/creativity of the model's output (0.0 = most deterministic). It affects WHAT text is produced, not WHETHER the model returns text versus a tool call - so it cannot enforce tool use.
sr: Parametar uzorkovanja koji kontroliše nasumičnost/kreativnost izlaza (0.0 = najdeterminističniji). Utiče na to KAKAV tekst nastaje, ne na to DA LI model vraća tekst ili poziv alata - zato ne može da iznudi korišćenje alata.

en: Passing the output of one (sub)agent as the input to another within a multi-agent system. The transmission format matters: structured data preserves fidelity, while free-form prose is lossy and ambiguous.
sr: Prenos izlaza jednog (sub)agenta kao ulaza drugom u multi-agent sistemu. Format prenosa je bitan: strukturirani podaci čuvaju tačnost, dok je slobodna proza lossy i dvosmislena.

en: A machine-readable output format (JSON, CSV) that preserves exact values and field semantics with no ambiguity. Preferred over free-form prose when data must be consumed by another program or agent.
sr: Mašinski-čitljiv izlazni format (JSON, CSV) koji čuva tačne vrednosti i značenje polja bez dvosmislenosti. Bolji od slobodne proze kada podatke konzumira drugi program ili agent.

en: A specialized agent to which a coordinator delegates a sub-task (coordinator/subagent architecture). Each subagent handles one focused job; the coordinator orchestrates and passes data between them.
sr: Specijalizovani agent kome koordinator delegira pod-zadatak (coordinator/subagent arhitektura). Svaki podagent radi jedan fokusiran posao; koordinator orkestrira i prenosi podatke između njih.

en: Forking as an abstract session-state primitive creates a new BRANCH of an existing session: it preserves the current context and produces a new session handle, enabling parallel/divergent workflows WITHOUT modifying the original (contrast --resume = continue same line, --clear-context = wipe). CAVEAT (per CCA-F): it is over-engineered when a clean data hand-off would suffice (d1-q005/07), AND - critically - it is NOT a documented mechanism for branching/sharing LIVE INTERACTIVE state BETWEEN HUMAN developers (d1-q018). For human collaboration/handoff the recommended approach is Git branches per task + a synthesized summary/Projects, not fork_session. Discriminator: abstract 'which op forks' (d1-q016, fork) vs human-to-human handoff (d1-q018, summarize+isolate).
sr: Forkovanje kao apstraktni session-state primitiv pravi novu GRANU postojeće sesije: čuva trenutni kontekst i daje nov session handle, omogućava paralelne/divergentne workflow-ove BEZ diranja originala (za razliku od --resume = nastavi istu liniju, --clear-context = obriši). CAVEAT (po CCA-F): preterano je kada bi čist prenos podataka bio dovoljan (d1-q005/07), I - ključno - NIJE dokumentovan mehanizam za grananje/deljenje ŽIVOG INTERAKTIVNOG stanja IZMEĐU LJUDSKIH developera (d1-q018). Za ljudsku saradnju/predaju preporuka je Git grane po zadatku + sintetizovan sažetak/Projects, ne fork_session. Diskriminator: apstraktno 'koja op forkuje' (d1-q016, fork) vs predaja između dvoje ljudi (d1-q018, summarize+isolate).

en: The model's internal reasoning, emitted as a text block before it acts (e.g. before a tool_use block). It is the logical bridge from the request to the action; preserving it in conversation history keeps multi-turn reasoning coherent.
sr: Interno rezonovanje modela, ispisano kao text blok pre nego što deluje (npr. pre tool_use bloka). To je logički most od zahteva do akcije; čuvanje u istoriji razgovora održava rezonovanje koherentnim kroz više koraka.

en: A content block type in an assistant response carrying prose/reasoning, as opposed to a tool_use block (a tool call). The text block before a tool_use typically holds the model's chain of thought - not redundant filler.
sr: Tip content bloka u odgovoru asistenta koji nosi prozu/rezonovanje, nasuprot tool_use bloku (poziv alata). Text blok ispred tool_use-a obično sadrži chain of thought modela - nije suvišni ukras.

en: The array of messages passed to the model on each request. The model is stateless: it only knows what is present in this history. Anything stripped or omitted does not exist for the model and cannot be regenerated.
sr: Niz messages koji se šalje modelu pri svakom zahtevu. Model je stateless: zna samo ono što je prisutno u ovoj istoriji. Sve što je izbačeno ili izostavljeno za model ne postoji i ne može biti regenerisano.

en: A subagent retains no memory between separate invocations. Each Task tool call starts fresh, so anything a prior invocation 'knew' (e.g. previously approved decisions) is gone unless re-supplied. Persisting context across calls is the coordinator's responsibility, not a built-in feature.
sr: Subagent ne pamti ništa između odvojenih poziva. Svaki Task poziv kreće 'od nule', pa sve što je prethodni poziv 'znao' (npr. ranije odobrene odluke) nestaje ako se ponovo ne prosledi. Čuvanje konteksta kroz pozive je odgovornost koordinatora, ne ugrađena funkcija.

en: The tool a coordinator uses to spawn/invoke a subagent and hand it a prompt. Each Task call is an independent invocation with no memory of previous ones - so all context the subagent needs must be included in that call's prompt.
sr: Alat kojim koordinator pokreće/poziva subagent i prosleđuje mu prompt. Svaki Task poziv je nezavisan i bez memorije prethodnih - pa sav kontekst koji subagentu treba mora biti u promptu tog poziva.

en: A retry strategy where the wait between attempts grows (e.g. 1s, 2s, 4s) instead of retrying immediately. Used for transient errors (503, timeout) to give the failing service time to recover without hammering it.
sr: Strategija ponavljanja gde pauza između pokušaja raste (npr. 1s, 2s, 4s) umesto trenutnog ponavljanja. Koristi se za tranzijentne greške (503, timeout) da se servisu da vremena da se oporavi, bez zatrpavanja.

en: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use: a flaw where the state checked at one moment changes before it is actually used. E.g. pinging an API for 200 OK (check) does not guarantee the real call (use) succeeds - it can fail in between.
sr: Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use: mana gde se stanje provereno u jednom trenutku promeni pre nego što se stvarno iskoristi. Npr. ping API-ja za 200 OK (provera) ne garantuje da ce pravi poziv (korišćenje) uspeti - može pasti u međuvremenu.

en: When an operation fails, returning a clear standardized signal (e.g. an 'Offline' status) instead of a raw error. The model then knows the true state and does not hallucinate or misinterpret a transport-level failure.
sr: Kad operacija ne uspe, vraćanje jasnog standardizovanog signala (npr. 'Offline' status) umesto sirove greške. Model tada zna stvarno stanje i ne halucinira niti pogrešno tumači grešku na transport nivou.

en: Subagents operate with isolated contexts: they do not share a global session state nor automatically inherit the coordinator's context. The coordinator must explicitly include any needed context in the delegation prompt. Multi-agent systems avoid auto-inheritance on purpose, to prevent token waste and context stuffing.
sr: Subagenti rade sa izolovanim kontekstom: ne dele globalno session state niti automatski nasleđuju koordinatorov kontekst. Koordinator mora eksplicitno da uključi sve potrebno u delegation prompt. Multi-agent sistemi namerno izbegavaju auto-nasleđivanje, da spreče token waste i context stuffing. Izolacija se koristi i namerno: forkovanje u izolovane niti (svaka sa sopstvenim context window-om) sprečava context pollution između nezavisnih istraga.

en: The prompt a coordinator sends when delegating a task to a subagent. Because the subagent inherits nothing, this prompt must carry ALL context the subagent needs (the relevant document, conversation history, constraints). Missing context here is the typical root cause of 'the subagent doesn't know X' failures.
sr: Prompt kojim koordinator delegira zadatak subagentu. Pošto subagent ništa ne nasleđuje, ovaj prompt mora da nosi SAV kontekst koji subagentu treba (relevantan dokument, istoriju razgovora, ograničenja). Nedostatak konteksta ovde je tipičan koren grešaka tipa 'subagent ne zna X'.

en: An orchestration topology where all subagents communicate THROUGH a central coordinator (the hub), never directly with each other (the spokes). The coordinator centralizes orchestration, error handling, validation, and retry/backoff, and gates each downstream step on the success of the upstream one. Contrast with brittle peer-to-peer wiring, where one agent's failure silently poisons the next.
sr: Topologija orkestracije gde svi subagenti komuniciraju KROZ centralnog koordinatora (hub), nikad direktno međusobno (spokes). Koordinator centralizuje orkestraciju, error handling, validaciju i retry/backoff, i gejtuje svaki naredni korak na uspeh prethodnog. Suprotnost krhkom peer-to-peer vezivanju, gde greška jednog agenta tiho zatruje sledećeg.

en: A design principle: each component/agent does its own focused job and does not take on another's responsibility. Violated when, e.g., a Summarizer agent is made to tail another agent's logs and run its own error-check logic - brittle and out of its lane. Keeps systems modular and debuggable.
sr: Dizajn princip: svaka komponenta/agent radi svoj fokusiran posao i ne preuzima tuđu odgovornost. Prekršen kad se npr. Summarizer natera da tail-uje tuđe logove i vodi sopstvenu error-check logiku - krhko i van njegove uloge. Čuva sistem modularnim i lakim za debug.

en: The moment a task is handed off to another executor (agent->human escalation, or agent->agent). A good handoff is STRUCTURED and CURATED - the cause, what was attempted, and the recommended next step - not a raw transcript, so the receiver continues rather than starting from scratch. Best enforced via a tool with required fields that forces the handing-off agent to synthesize the needed context.
sr: Trenutak predaje zadatka drugom izvršiocu (agent->čovek eskalacija ili agent->agent). Dobra predaja je STRUKTURISANA i KURIRANA - uzrok, šta je pokušano, preporučeni sledeći korak - ne sirov transkript, da primalac nastavi umesto da krene iz nule. Najbolje iznuđena tool-om sa obaveznim poljima koji primorava agenta-predavaoca da sintetizuje potreban kontekst.

en: A pattern (HITL) where a human approves an agent's actions before they execute. The right tool for destructive actions or final approvals - NOT for every diagnostic step, where it adds latency and defeats automation. Solves control/safety, not handoff quality.
sr: Obrazac (HITL) gde čovek odobrava akcije agenta pre izvršenja. Pravi alat za destruktivne akcije ili finalna odobrenja - NE za svaki dijagnostički korak, gde uvodi latenciju i guši automatizaciju. Rešava kontrolu/bezbednost, ne kvalitet predaje.

en: A failure mode where a single agent juggling too many parallel tasks and tools dilutes its focus: it gets confused, drops tasks, and exceeds its reasoning limits. The fix is not a bigger token window or a faster model but decomposition into focused subagents under a coordinator - the bottleneck is orchestration/cognitive load, not capacity or speed.
sr: Režim otkaza gde jedan agent žongliranjem previše paralelnih zadataka i tool-ova razvodnjava fokus: zbunjuje se, ispušta zadatke, prebija reasoning limite. Lek nije veći token prozor ni brzi model, nego dekompozicija na fokusirane subagente pod koordinatorom - bottleneck je orkestracija/kognitivni teret, ne kapacitet ni brzina.

en: When too much content is packed into a single context/prompt, the model's attention spreads thin and it loses or fabricates details (hallucinations). The reason a single giant prompt fails on long input; chunking mitigates it, but reverting to one prompt (even with strict XML tags) brings the problem back - tags do not reduce the amount of context.
sr: Kad se previše sadržaja ugura u jedan kontekst/prompt, pažnja modela se razvodni pa gubi ili izmišlja detalje (halucinacije). Razlog zašto jedan ogroman prompt pada na dugačkom ulazu; chunking ublažava, ali vraćanje na jedan prompt (čak i sa strogim XML tagovima) vraća problem - tagovi ne smanjuju količinu konteksta.

en: A decomposition pattern: 'map' runs local passes over independent chunks; 'reduce' (an integration/synthesis pass) reasons over the chunks' OUTPUTS/summaries to derive global conclusions. Chunking alone (map without reduce) catches local issues but misses cross-chunk/global patterns; the reduce step bridges the context gap. More parallelism is NOT the fix - the integration step is.
sr: Obrazac dekompozicije: 'map' = lokalni prolazi po nezavisnim chunk-ovima; 'reduce' (integration/synthesis pass) = rezonuje nad IZLAZIMA/sažecima chunk-ova da izvede globalne zaključke. Chunking sam (map bez reduce) hvata lokalne probleme ali promašuje cross-chunk/globalne; reduce premošćuje context gap. Više paralelizma NIJE lek - integration korak jeste.

en: A dedicated tool (e.g. research_complete(), submit_final_answer()) that the model CALLS to explicitly signal a TASK is complete; the loop detects that tool_use block by name. This is the reliable, schema-validated completion signal - preferred over end_turn (which only means the turn ended, not the task) and over natural-language parsing or stop_sequence (which depend on the model emitting exact text).
sr: Namenski tool (npr. research_complete(), submit_final_answer()) koji model POZOVE da eksplicitno signalizira da je ZADATAK gotov; petlja detektuje taj tool_use blok po imenu. To je pouzdan, schema-validisan signal završetka - bolji od end_turn (koji znači samo da je potez završen, ne zadatak) i od NL parsiranja ili stop_sequence (koji zavise od toga da model emituje tačan tekst).

en: A custom token sequence that, when generated by the model, halts generation at the API level (and is reported back as the stop_reason). A real mechanism, but a poor choice for signaling task completion: it still depends on the model EMITTING that exact sequence (natural-language generation), so it is not deterministic control. Anthropic recommends structured tool calls over stop sequences for completion signals.
sr: Prilagođeni niz tokena koji, kad ga model generise, prekida generisanje na nivou API-ja (i vraća se kao stop_reason). Realan mehanizam, ali loš izbor za signaliziranje završetka zadatka: i dalje zavisi od toga da model EMITUJE baš taj niz (generisanje prirodnog jezika), pa nije deterministička kontrola. Anthropic preporučuje strukturne tool pozive umesto stop sekvenci za signale završetka.

en: Returning to an existing saved session and continuing from its last state (e.g. --resume <session-name>). It CONTINUES the same line of interaction; it does not branch it. Distinct from forking (creates a divergent branch from the state) and from clearing context (wipes/resets the state).
sr: Vraćanje u postojeću sačuvanu sesiju i nastavak iz njenog poslednjeg stanja (npr. --resume <session-name>). NASTAVLJA istu liniju interakcije; ne grana je. Razlikuj od forkovanja (pravi divergentnu granu iz stanja) i od clear-context (briše/resetuje stanje).

en: An orchestration pattern where the Coordinator evaluates a query's complexity/intent UPFRONT and dynamically dispatches it to only the relevant subagent(s) ('fast path'), bypassing the rest - instead of pushing every query through a fixed full pipeline. Matches work to need: lower latency and compute without sacrificing accuracy. Contrast with broadcasting to all + taking the first response (fast but risks an inaccurate answer).
sr: Obrazac orkestracije gde Coordinator UNAPRED proceni složenost/nameru upita i dinamički ga pošalje SAMO relevantnom subagentu/ima ('fast path'), preskacuci ostale - umesto da svaki upit gura kroz fiksni puni pipeline. Upari posao sa potrebom: manje latencije i compute-a bez žrtvovanja tačnosti. Suprotno od broadcast-a svima + uzimanja prvog odgovora (brzo ali rizikuje netačan odgovor).

en: An Anthropic feature for sharing knowledge/context across a team or a set of conversations. The recommended vehicle for human collaboration and context sharing: a developer starts a new, isolated session bootstrapped with synthesized context (e.g. a Claude-generated summary of the existing schema/resolvers), rather than sharing or forking one live interactive session between people. Pairs with isolating agent scope and Git branches per task to avoid conflicts.
sr: Anthropic feature za deljenje znanja/konteksta kroz tim ili skup razgovora. Preporučeno sredstvo za ljudsku saradnju i context sharing: developer krene u novoj, IZOLOVANOJ sesiji bootstrap-ovanoj sintetizovanim kontekstom (npr. Claude-generisan sažetak postojeće schema/resolvera), umesto deljenja ili forkovanja jedne žive interaktivne sesije između ljudi. Ide uz izolaciju scope-a agenta i Git grane po zadatku da se izbegnu konflikti.

en: A content block returned in a USER message carrying the output of an executed tool, paired to its originating tool_use block via tool_use_id. When the assistant made multiple (parallel) tool_use calls, all the tool_result blocks go together in ONE user message. tool_result does NOT belong in a system or assistant message.
sr: Content blok koji se vraća u USER poruci i nosi izlaz izvršenog alata, uparen sa svojim izvornim tool_use blokom preko tool_use_id. Kad je asistent uputio više (paralelnih) tool_use poziva, svi tool_result blokovi idu zajedno u JEDNU user poruku. tool_result NE pripada system ni assistant poruci. Mora biti STRUKTURNI blok (ne plain text) - inače puca tool_use_id veza i model gubi proveniencu/stanje.

en: The Messages API requirement that 'user' and 'assistant' roles strictly alternate - you cannot have two consecutive messages of the same role. This is why tool_use (in the assistant message) and all its corresponding tool_result blocks (in one following user message) preserve the alternation; splitting results across several user messages would create consecutive user messages and break the pattern.
sr: Zahtev Messages API-ja da se 'user' i 'assistant' role-ovi strogo smenjuju - ne možeš imati dve uzastopne poruke istog role-a. Zato tool_use (u assistant poruci) i svi pripadajući tool_result blokovi (u jednoj sledećoj user poruci) čuvaju alternaciju; razbijanje rezultata u više user poruka napravilo bi uzastopne user poruke i pokvarilo obrazac.

en: Anthropic models can emit MULTIPLE tool_use blocks in a single assistant turn (calling several tools at once). All their results are returned together in one user message. Supported by default; the disable_parallel_tool_use option forces one-tool-at-a-time, but is not required just because multiple tools were called - the results are simply batched.
sr: Anthropic modeli mogu emitovati VIŠE tool_use blokova u jednom assistant potezu (više alata odjednom). Svi rezultati se vraćaju zajedno u jednoj user poruci. Podržano po defaultu; opcija disable_parallel_tool_use forsira jedan-po-jedan, ali nije potrebna samo zato što je pozvano više alata - rezultati se prosto grupišu.

en: An identifier that links a tool_result back to its originating tool_use call. It provides PROVENANCE - letting the model reliably map each observation (result) to the specific action (call) that produced it. It exists only inside a structured tool_result block; appending the result as plain text drops the tool_use_id, breaking the action<->result link and causing hallucination and loss of state.
sr: Identifikator koji povezuje tool_result sa njegovim originalnim tool_use pozivom. Daje PROVENIENCU - model pouzdano mapira svaku opservaciju (rezultat) na tačnu akciju (poziv) koja ju je proizvela. Postoji samo unutar strukturnog tool_result bloka; lepljenje rezultata kao plain text izbacuje tool_use_id, kida vezu akcija<->ishod i izaziva halucinaciju i gubitak stanja.

en: Accumulation of IRRELEVANT or stale data in the context window that corrupts subsequent reasoning (e.g. network logs from a failed DDoS investigation confusing a later database-failure investigation). Distinct from attention dilution (that is about too MUCH content/volume; this is about irrelevant content bleeding in and contaminating). Fix: fork into isolated threads after a shared setup so each line of work has a clean, independent context.
sr: Nakupljanje IRELEVANTNIH ili zastarelih podataka u context window-u koji kvare naredno rezonovanje (npr. mrežni logovi iz neuspešne DDoS istrage zbunjuju kasniju DB istragu). Razlika od attention dilution (tamo: previše sadržaja/volumen; ovde: irelevantan sadržaj curi i kontaminira). Lek: forkovati u izolovane niti posle zajedničkog setupa, da svaka linija rada ima čist, nezavisan kontekst.

en: AWS service providing virtual servers (instances) in the cloud, billed per running time. In agentic cost-optimization scenarios, EC2 instances are typically the largest spend item an agent starts from - but an instance is rarely standalone; its lifecycle may be controlled by an Auto Scaling Group.
sr: AWS servis koji daje virtuelne servere (instance) u oblaku, naplata po vremenu rada. U agentskim scenarijima optimizacije troškova, EC2 instance su obično najveća stavka od koje agent kreće - ali instanca retko stoji sama; njenim životnim ciklusom može upravljati Auto Scaling Group.

en: An AWS mechanism that automatically launches and terminates EC2 instances according to min/max/desired and scaling rules - effectively the 'owner' of those instances. Implication for an agent: terminating an instance without understanding its ASG can be futile (the ASG re-launches it) or harmful (it disrupts something the ASG maintains). A discovered, undocumented ASG is therefore in-scope context to investigate, not scope creep to ignore.
sr: AWS mehanizam koji automatski pali i gasi EC2 instance prema min/max/željenom broju i scaling pravilima - praktično 'vlasnik' tih instanci. Posledica za agenta: gašenje instance bez razumevanja njenog ASG-a može biti uzaludno (ASG je ponovo upali) ili štetno (poremeti nešto što ASG održava). Otkriven, nedokumentovan ASG je zato kontekst koji treba istražiti, a ne 'scope creep' koji se ignoriše.

en: EU data protection law requiring that personal data of EU residents physically stay within permitted (EU) geographies; certain data cannot be transferred to US regions. A hard legal constraint, not a best-effort goal.
sr: Evropski zakon o zaštiti podataka: podaci o EU građanima moraju fizički ostati u dozvoljenoj (EU) geografiji i ne smeju u US regione. Tvrdo PRAVNO ograničenje, ne 'best effort' - kršenje znači kaznu, pa je determinisana prevencija (PreToolCall hook) jedina garancija.

en: AWS Simple Storage Service top-level container for files/objects. Each bucket lives in a specific AWS region (a geographic data-center location), which is why moving a bucket between regions has data-residency implications.
sr: Kontejner najvišeg nivoa za fajlove u AWS S3 skladištu. Svaki bucket živi u jednom REGIONU (geografska lokacija data centra), zato premeštanje bucket-a između regiona dodiruje data residency. U pitanju alat transfer_bucket baš to radi.

en: AWS Identity and Access Management - the permission system controlling who (which identity) may perform which action on which resource. Can block actions, but is generally not granular enough to judge data residency based on a bucket's content.
sr: AWS Identity and Access Management - sistem dozvola: ko sme koju akciju nad kojim resursom. Može da blokira akcije, ali nije dovoljno granularan da procenjuje residency po SADRŽAJU bucket-a (da li baš taj bucket ima EU podatke), pa sam po sebi ne garantuje GDPR usklađenost.

en: A final-step agent (aka aggregator) that takes the structured outputs of multiple parallel sub-agents and produces a single, coherent natural-language response addressing the whole query. The recommended way to merge fan-out results.
sr: Agent završnog koraka (sinonim: aggregator) koji uzme strukturisane izlaze više paralelnih pod-agenata i napravi JEDAN koherentan odgovor na prirodnom jeziku za celo pitanje. Preporučeni način spajanja fan-out rezultata; koordinacija ide preko njega, ne peer-to-peer.

en: An orchestration component that inspects an incoming request and dispatches sub-tasks to the appropriate sub-agents (often fanning out to several in parallel). It routes work; it is not where final synthesis belongs.
sr: Komponenta orkestracije koja pregleda zahtev i prosledi pod-zadatke pravim pod-agentima (često fan-out u paralelu). Ruti posao; finalna sinteza NIJE njegov posao (za to ide synthesizer). Odbacivanje sporijeg pod-agenta radi latencije je anti-obrazac.

en: An anti-pattern in which sub-agents talk directly to each other (e.g. via shared memory) to negotiate a result, instead of being coordinated through a central aggregator/synthesizer. Adds coupling and complexity with no convergence guarantee.
sr: Anti-obrazac u kome pod-agenti komuniciraju direktno među sobom (npr. preko deljene memorije) da 'ispregovaraju' rezultat, umesto da ih koordiniše centralni aggregator/synthesizer. Dodaje coupling i kompleksnost, bez garancije konvergencije. Robusna orkestracija = hub-and-spoke.

en: The list of tools an agent is permitted to call. Putting a tool here makes it AVAILABLE but does not make the model USE it — the system prompt must still tell the model when to invoke it.
sr: Lista alata koje agent SME da pozove. Stavljanje alata ovde ga čini DOSTUPNIM, ali ne znači da ce ga model UPOTREBITI - sistem prompt i dalje mora reći modelu KADA da ga pozove. Dostupan alat != upotrebljen alat.

en: The definition of a subagent: its identity, system prompt, and the tools it may use. It describes the subagent; it does NOT contain an automatic routing trigger that fires it.
sr: Definicija subagenta: identitet, sistem prompt i alati koje sme da koristi. OPISUJE subagenta; ne sadrži automatski okidač (npr. izmišljeni 'trigger_intent') koji bi ga sam pokrenuo - invokaciju radi koordinator preko Task alata.

en: The orchestrating main agent that delegates sub-tasks to specialized subagents (by calling the Task tool). Coordination flows through it (hub-and-spoke), not peer-to-peer between subagents.
sr: Glavni agent koji orkestrira i delegira pod-zadatke specijalizovanim subagentima (pozivom Task alata). Koordinacija ide kroz njega (hub-and-spoke), ne peer-to-peer među subagentima. Da bi delegirao, prompt mu mora reći KADA da pozove Task.

en: The binding 'this datum came from this source'. In multi-agent pipelines it must survive every handoff. Best preserved by passing structured data that explicitly links each item to its source metadata, not prose the downstream agent must parse.
sr: Veza 'ovaj podatak potiče iz ovog izvora'. U više-agentskim pipeline-ima mora preživi svaki handoff. Najbolje se čuva prenosom STRUKTURNIH podataka koji eksplicitno vezuju svaku stavku za metapodatke izvora (doc/strana/URL), a ne prozom koju nizvodni agent mora da parsira (rizik misatribucije/halucinacije).

en: A local rate-limiting mechanism: a bucket holds N tokens, each call consumes one, tokens refill over time; when empty, calls are blocked or made to wait. Enforced in code before a request leaves, so it can deterministically prevent exceeding an API limit.
sr: Lokalni mehanizam ograničavanja brzine: kofica ima N žetona, svaki poziv troši jedan, žetoni se obnavljaju u vremenu; kad nema žetona -> blokiraj/čekaj. Sprovodi se u KODU pre nego zahtev ode, pa deterministički sprečava prekoračenje API limita. Stavlja se u PreToolCall hook.

en: An API rule capping requests per unit of time. Exceeding it triggers a penalty (here a 24h account ban). Because the penalty is immediate/irreversible, protection must PREVENT the offending call before it is sent (PreToolCall), not react after.
sr: Pravilo API-ja koje ograničava broj zahteva po jedinici vremena. Prekoračenje kažnjava (ovde 24h ban naloga). Pošto je kazna trenutna/nepovratna, zaštita mora da SPREČI sporni poziv pre slanja (PreToolCall), a ne da reaguje posle (PostToolCall je prekasno).

en: Converting heterogeneous inputs/outputs into a single consistent format before the model interprets them. Done deterministically in code (e.g., a PostToolUse hook over the tool result), not via prompt instructions, so the model never has to reconcile ambiguous raw values.
sr: Svođenje raznorodnih ulaza/izlaza na jedan dosledan format pre nego ih model tumači. Radi se DETERMINISTIČKI u kodu (npr. PostToolUse hook nad rezultatom alata), ne uputstvom u promptu, da model nikad ne mora da pomiruje dvosmislene sirove vrednosti (npr. 0/200/OK kao 'uspeh').

en: The agreed target shape that all variants are mapped to (e.g., {"status":"SUCCESS"}). Removes ambiguity: different legacy codes (0, 200, OK) all become the same normalized structure the model can read reliably.
sr: Dogovoreni ciljni oblik u koji se sve varijante mapiraju (npr. {'status':'SUCCESS'}). Uklanja dvosmislenost: različiti legacy kodovi (0, 200, OK) postaju ista normalizovana struktura koju model pouzdano čita.

en: An Anthropic Structured Outputs feature: setting strict: true on a tool definition guarantees the model's tool inputs match the provided input_schema (JSON Schema) by constraining token sampling to schema-valid outputs (constrained decoding) - the invalid call is never generated. Supported on Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5 (and legacy Opus 4.5/4.1). Supports format/enum/const/anyOf/$ref/additionalProperties:false; does NOT enforce 'pattern' regex or minLength/maxLength (string constraints are stripped/validated client-side). Use format:'date' for a date, not a regex pattern.
sr: Anthropic Structured Outputs funkcija: strict: true u definiciji alata garantuje da tool inputs modela prate zadati input_schema (JSON Schema) tako što ograničava token sampling na schema-validne izlaze (constrained decoding) - nevažeći poziv nikad ne nastane. Garanciju daje API parametar, ne prompt. Podržano na Opus 4.8 / Sonnet 4.6 / Haiku 4.5 (i legacy Opus 4.5/4.1). Podržava format/enum/const/anyOf/$ref/additionalProperties:false; NE iznuđuje 'pattern' regex ni minLength/maxLength (string constraints se uklanjaju/validiraju klijentski). Za datum koristi format:'date', ne regex pattern.

en: A coordinator/orchestrator agent that holds no domain tools of its own; it reads the user's query and routes it to specialized subagents. A naming variant of the coordinator/hub-and-spoke role, emphasized in tool-distribution designs.
sr: Koordinator/orkestrator agent koji sam nema domenske alate; čita korisnikov upit i rutira ga specijalizovanim subagentima. Imenska varijanta coordinator/hub-and-spoke uloge, naglašena u dizajnu raspodele alata.

en: The principle of spreading tools across multiple specialized agents, each with a small focused set (2-3 related tools), instead of giving one agent all tools. Reduces decision complexity and wrong-tool selection; pairs with a routing Supervisor.
sr: Princip raspodele alata na više specijalizovanih agenata, svaki sa malim fokusiranim skupom (2-3 srodna alata), umesto da jedan agent ima sve alate. Smanjuje decision complexity i pogrešan izbor alata; ide uz routing Supervisor.

en: Model Context Protocol - an open protocol that standardizes how a host/client (e.g. Claude Code, an agent) connects to external servers exposing tools, resources, and prompts. The client maintains persistent connections to all configured servers and aggregates their tools into one namespace.
sr: Model Context Protocol - otvoreni protokol koji standardizuje kako se host/klijent (npr. Claude Code, agent) povezuje na spoljne servere koji izlažu alate, resurse i promptove. Klijent drži trajne konekcije ka svim konfigurisanim serverima i skuplja njihove alate u jedan namespace.

en: A process exposing tools/resources/prompts over MCP (via stdio or HTTP/SSE transport). Multiple MCP servers can be connected concurrently; each is modular and domain-specific (e.g. a Market Data server, a Trade Execution server).
sr: Proces koji izlaže alate/resurse/promptove preko MCP-a (stdio ili HTTP/SSE transport). Više MCP servera može biti povezano istovremeno; svaki je modularan i domenski specifičan (npr. Market Data server, Trade Execution server).

en: A project-scoped configuration file that declares which MCP servers a project connects to (command/transport/args). On session start the client reads it, connects to every listed server, and discovers their tools.
sr: Konfiguracioni fajl na nivou projekta koji deklariše na koje MCP servere se projekat povezuje (komanda/transport/argumenti). Pri pokretanju sesije klijent ga pročita, poveže se na svaki naveden server i otkrije njihove alate.

en: The step where, after connecting and performing the initialize handshake, the MCP client queries each server (tools/list) to learn its available tools. Done once at connection time; the discovered tools then remain available for the session.
sr: Korak u kome, nakon povezivanja i initialize handshake-a, MCP klijent upita svaki server (tools/list) da sazna dostupne alate. Radi se jednom pri konekciji; otkriveni alati zatim ostaju dostupni kroz sesiju.

en: A connection that the MCP host keeps open for the whole session after the initial handshake, rather than re-establishing it per tool call. Enables calling tools across multiple servers without re-connect/switching latency.
sr: Konekcija koju MCP host drži otvorenom kroz celu sesiju nakon početnog handshake-a, umesto da je uspostavlja iznova pred svaki poziv alata. Omogućava pozivanje alata sa više servera bez latencije ponovnog povezivanja/prebacivanja.

en: A boolean field on an MCP tool result indicating whether the tool call failed. isError: true tells the model the operation did not succeed (so it can retry, escalate, or surface the failure); isError: false signals success. Swallowing a backend failure and returning isError: false masks the error from the agent.
sr: Boolean polje na rezultatu MCP alata koje označava da li je poziv alata pao. isError: true govori modelu da operacija NIJE uspela (pa može da retry-uje, eskalira ili prikaže grešku); isError: false signalizira uspeh. Gutanje backend greške i vraćanje isError: false skriva grešku od agenta.

en: Returning a tool failure as an explicit, structured signal (isError: true plus a descriptive message) rather than as a silent 'success with empty result'. Lets the agent distinguish 'no data found' from 'the call failed' and react accordingly. The opposite anti-pattern swallows exceptions and reports false success.
sr: Vraćanje neuspeha alata kao eksplicitnog, strukturisanog signala (isError: true uz opisnu poruku) umesto kao tihog 'uspeh + prazan rezultat'. Omogućava agentu da razlikuje 'nema podataka' od 'poziv je pao' i da reaguje. Suprotni anti-pattern guta izuzetke i prijavljuje lažan uspeh.

en: A result that reports 'nothing found' when something actually exists. In agentic tooling this arises when a failed call is masked as a successful empty result - the model concludes 'all clear' while a real failure (e.g. expired credentials) went undetected. Critically dangerous in security/threat detection.
sr: Rezultat koji javlja 'ničeg nema' iako nešto zapravo postoji. U agentskom toolingu nastaje kad se neuspeo poziv maskira kao uspešan prazan rezultat - model zaključi 'čisto' dok je stvarni neuspeh (npr. istekli kredencijali) ostao neprimećen. Posebno opasno u security/detekciji pretnji.

en: An orchestration technique where the output of one tool becomes the input of another (e.g. geocode_address → calculate_distance). Lets the model handle complex multi-step tasks and obtain data deterministically instead of hallucinating it, improving reliability.
sr: Tehnika orkestracije gde izlaz jednog alata postaje ulaz drugog (npr. geocode_address → calculate_distance). Omogućava modelu da reši složene multi-step zadatke i pribavi podatak deterministički umesto da ga halucinira, čime raste pouzdanost.

en: Examples of correct tool calls included in a tool definition. They guide the model toward proper usage and the expected input format, reducing malformed or hallucinated parameters - part of designing a reliable tool interface.
sr: Primeri ispravnih poziva alata uključeni u definiciju alata. Vode model ka pravilnom korišćenju i očekivanom formatu ulaza, smanjuju loše formirane ili halucinirane parametre - deo dizajna pouzdanog tool interfejsa.

en: The natural-language description of a tool's purpose, behavior, and boundaries in its definition. Clear, specific descriptions are central to reliable tool use - they tell the model when and how to use the tool and what its inputs mean.
sr: Opis namene, ponašanja i granica alata na prirodnom jeziku u njegovoj definiciji. Jasni, precizni opisi su ključni za pouzdan tool use - govore modelu kada i kako da koristi alat i šta njegovi ulazi znače.

en: When the model fabricates data it does not actually know (e.g. inventing precise coordinates from a street address), producing plausible but wrong values. The fix is to give the model a tool to fetch the data rather than relying on it to generate it; loosening schemas only invites more hallucination.
sr: Kad model fabrikuje podatke koje zapravo ne zna (npr. izmišlja precizne koordinate iz adrese), proizvodeći uverljive ali pogrešne vrednosti. Rešenje je dati modelu alat da pribavi podatak umesto da se oslanja na njegovo generisanje; labavljenje šema samo poziva još halucinacija.

en: Prompt instructions whose key words unintentionally steer the model - e.g. 'Execute...' biases the model toward a tool with 'execute' in its name. Anthropic warns these create unintended tool associations; mitigate with clear, structured prompts and explicit tool guidance.
sr: Instrukcije u promptu čije ključne reči nenamerno usmeravaju model - npr. 'Execute...' navodi model ka alatu sa 'execute' u imenu. Anthropic upozorava da stvaraju nenamerne asocijacije sa alatima; ublažava se jasnim, strukturiranim promptovima i eksplicitnim uputstvom o alatima.

en: When prompt phrasing links a word to a tool and causes wrong tool selection - the system prompt's wording effectively overrides the tool descriptions. The misrouting is systematic (tied to wording), not random.
sr: Kad formulacija prompta poveže reč sa alatom i izazove pogrešan izbor alata - formulacija system prompta praktično pregazi opise alata. Misrouting je sistematski (vezan za formulaciju), ne nasumičan.

en: An explicit prohibition in a tool description or prompt (e.g. 'do not execute live trades'). It can help disambiguate, but it does not fix the root cause when the real problem is keyword-sensitive prompt wording that primes the wrong tool.
sr: Eksplicitna zabrana u opisu alata ili promptu (npr. 'ne izvršavaj live trade'). Može pomoći u razdvajanju, ali ne rešava koren kad je pravi problem keyword-sensitivna formulacija prompta koja primira pogrešan alat.

en: A pre-built, community-maintained MCP server for a popular system or SaaS platform (e.g. Shopify, Zendesk). Reusing one instead of building from scratch reduces implementation effort and speeds up delivery.
sr: Gotov, community-održavan MCP server za popularan sistem ili SaaS platformu (npr. Shopify, Zendesk). Reuse umesto gradnje iz nule smanjuje napor implementacije i ubrzava isporuku.

en: An MCP server you build yourself, warranted when no community server exists or the system is proprietary/non-standard (e.g. a legacy AS/400 mainframe). Lets you focus custom effort only where robust integration genuinely requires it.
sr: MCP server koji sam gradiš, opravdan kad nema community rešenja ili je sistem proprietaran/nestandardan (npr. legacy AS/400 mainframe). Omogućava da custom napor usmeriš samo tamo gde robusna integracija stvarno to traži.

en: An old, often proprietary system (e.g. an AS/400 mainframe) that typically requires specialized protocol handling, custom business logic, or specific adapters. A generic REST wrapper usually cannot integrate with it robustly, so it warrants a custom MCP server.
sr: Star, često proprietaran sistem (npr. AS/400 mainframe) koji obično traži specijalizovan protocol handling, custom poslovnu logiku ili specifične adaptere. Generički REST wrapper najčešće ne može robustno da se integriše s njim, pa zahteva custom MCP server.

en: A single server that wraps multiple unrelated systems into one unit. An anti-pattern for MCP integration: it creates unnecessary coupling, a single point of failure, and is harder to test/maintain - and it ignores available community servers. Prefer one modular server per system.
sr: Jedan server koji umota više nesrodnih sistema u jednu celinu. Anti-pattern za MCP integraciju: stvara nepotrebno kuplovanje, single point of failure, teži je za test/održavanje - i ignoriše dostupne community servere. Bolje zaseban modularni server po sistemu.

en: A narrow, purpose-built tool of limited scope given to an agent for exactly its frequent, specific need (e.g. a verify_fact tool for the Writer). Proper tool scoping - preferable to handing over a full suite of powerful tools and restricting usage via prompts (which is fragile).
sr: Uzak, namenski alat ograničenog opsega dat agentu baš za njegovu čestu, specifičnu potrebu (npr. verify_fact za Writer-a). Pravo tool scoping - bolje nego dati pun skup moćnih alata pa ograničavati korišćenje promptom (što je krhko).

en: How much the model must hold and choose among (tools, context) while working. A bloated agent with many irrelevant tools raises cognitive load and can reduce accuracy on simple tasks - an argument for scoped tools and separation of concerns over a single mega-agent.
sr: Koliko model mora da „drži" i bira (alati, kontekst) dok radi. Bloated agent sa mnogo nerelevantnih alata diže cognitive load i može smanjiti tačnost na prostim zadacima - argument za scoped alate i separaciju poslova umesto jednog mega-agenta.

en: A multi-agent pattern where one agent generates content (e.g. code) and a separate agent validates it (e.g. runs tests). Splitting generation from validation enforces separation of concerns and prevents an agent from performing tasks outside its role.
sr: Multi-agent obrazac u kome jedan agent generiše sadržaj (npr. kod), a drugi ga validira (npr. pokreće testove). Razdvajanje generisanja od validacije sprovodi separaciju poslova i sprečava agenta da radi posao van svoje uloge.

en: A multi-agent architecture where an orchestrator splits a complex task among specialized worker agents. It leverages specialization rather than one monolithic agent; merging workers into a single agent eliminates its benefits.
sr: Multi-agent arhitektura u kojoj orkestrator deli složen zadatak specijalizovanim worker agentima. Oslanja se na specijalizaciju umesto jednog monolitnog agenta; spajanje worker-a u jedan agent briše te benefite.

en: The principle that each agent does a narrow, clearly defined job with its own toolset. It is a foundation of multi-agent architecture and the opposite of a bloated monolithic agent that holds every tool.
sr: Princip da svaki agent radi uzak, jasno definisan posao sa svojim skupom alata. Temelj multi-agent arhitekture i suprotnost bloated monolitnom agentu koji drži sve alate.

en: A failure mode where an agent gets stuck repeating the same action - e.g. a tool_choice that forces the same tool on every turn makes it call that tool with the same payload over and over after an error. Broken by returning freedom (tool_choice auto), changing state, or adding a stop condition.
sr: Režim otkaza u kome agent zaglavi ponavljajući istu akciju - npr. tool_choice koji forsira isti alat na svakom turn-u tera ga da zove taj alat sa istim payload-om iznova posle greške. Prekida se vraćanjem slobode (tool_choice auto), promenom stanja ili stop-uslovom.

en: Claude Code built-in tool that reads a file's contents (whole or a portion). Required before Edit. Used for selective/sequential context building rather than bulk-reading many files.
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alat koji čita sadržaj fajla (ceo ili deo). Obavezan pre Edit-a. Koristi se za selektivno/sekvencijalno građenje konteksta umesto bulk-čitanja mnogo fajlova.

en: Claude Code built-in tool that searches file CONTENTS by regex (ripgrep-based), returning matching files/lines. Use it to locate where something appears in code (e.g. a login entry point) - contrast with Glob (by name).
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alat koji pretražuje SADRŽAJ fajlova po regex-u (zasnovan na ripgrep), vraća fajlove/linije koje se poklapaju. Koristi ga da nađeš gde se nešto pojavljuje u kodu (npr. login ulazna tačka) - suprotno od Glob-a (po imenu).

en: Claude Code built-in tool that finds files by NAME/path using glob patterns (e.g. **/*auth*.*). It can filter by filename; it does NOT search file contents (that's Grep).
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alat koji pronalazi fajlove po IMENU/putanji glob patternima (npr. **/*auth*.*). Može da filtrira po imenu fajla; NE pretražuje sadržaj (to je Grep).

en: Claude Code built-in tool that makes a targeted change to an existing file by exact-string replacement. Requires a prior Read. Meant for modification, not for tracing/understanding code.
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alat koji pravi ciljanu izmenu postojećeg fajla zamenom tačnog stringa. Zahteva prethodni Read. Služi za modifikaciju, ne za tracing/razumevanje koda.

en: Claude Code built-in tool that runs shell commands (e.g. find, git, npm). Used for everything outside direct file read/edit operations.
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alat koji pokreće shell komande (npr. find, git, npm). Koristi se za sve van direktnih fajl read/edit operacija.

en: The limited span of context the model can hold at once. Bulk-reading many files floods it with mostly-irrelevant text, degrading performance and raising token usage - hence targeted Grep + sequential Read instead.
sr: Ograničen prostor konteksta koji model može da drži odjednom. Bulk-čitanje mnogo fajlova ga zatrpa uglavnom nerelevantnim tekstom, degradira performanse i diže token usage - zato ciljani Grep + sekvencijalni Read umesto toga.

en: The built-in Claude Code tools (Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob). Choose by job: Grep/Glob/Read for discovery, Edit/Write for modification, Bash for everything else. Subdomain 2.5 is about applying them effectively.
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alati (Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Grep, Glob). Biraju se po poslu: Grep/Glob/Read za discovery, Edit/Write za izmenu, Bash za ostalo. Subdomain 2.5 je o njihovoj efektivnoj primeni.

en: Claude Code built-in tool that writes or overwrites an entire file (new file or full rewrite). Distinct from Edit, which makes a targeted in-place change.
sr: Ugrađeni Claude Code alat koji piše ili prepisuje ceo fajl (nov fajl ili pun rewrite). Razlikuje se od Edit-a, koji pravi ciljanu izmenu na licu mesta.

en: An MCP mechanism for exposing structured, read-only data/context to the agent (e.g. a database schema catalog). It acts as an authoritative source of truth the model can reference on demand, removing exploratory tool calls and preventing hallucination. MCP exposes tools, resources, and prompts.
sr: MCP mehanizam za izlaganje strukturisanog, read-only podatka/konteksta agentu (npr. schema katalog baze). Deluje kao authoritative source of truth koji model konsultuje po potrebi, ukida eksplorativne pozive alata i sprečava halucinaciju. MCP izlaže tools, resources i prompts.

en: Tool calls made just to DISCOVER information before the real work (e.g. DESCRIBE TABLE, ls/Glob, Read to inspect structure). They cost turns, latency, and tokens, and often number in the dozens; eliminated by providing the data upfront as a resource.
sr: Pozivi alata koji se prave samo da bi se OTKRILA informacija pre pravog posla (npr. DESCRIBE TABLE, ls/Glob, Read radi provere strukture). Troše turn-ove, latenciju i token-e, često ih ima na desetine; ukidaju se davanjem podatka unapred kao resource.

en: A Claude Code plugin: a package that adds commands, agents, MCP servers, or hooks to a project. Enabled/disabled via `claude plugin enable/disable <name>@<marketplace>` or the /plugin UI.
sr: Claude Code plugin: paket koji projektu dodaje komande, agente, MCP servere ili hooks. Uključuje se/isključuje preko `claude plugin enable/disable <ime>@<marketplace>` ili /plugin UI-ja.

en: Dependencies one Claude Code plugin declares on others in plugin.json. Claude Code is dependency-aware: it refuses to disable a plugin while another enabled plugin depends on it, naming the dependents and offering a chained command to disable them in order.
sr: Zavisnosti koje jedan Claude Code plugin deklariše na druge u plugin.json. Claude Code je dependency-aware: odbija disable plugina dok neki enabled plugin zavisi od njega, imenuje zavisne i nudi chained command da ih isključi po redu.

en: A Claude Code plugin's manifest in the .claude-plugin/ directory. Holds metadata and a `dependencies` array (a bare name string, or an object {name, version} with a version constraint).
sr: Manifest Claude Code plugina u .claude-plugin/ direktorijumu. Sadrži metapodatke i `dependencies` niz (golo ime kao string, ili objekat {name, version} sa ograničenjem verzije).

en: Anthropic best-practice: restrict a tool's capability to a validated, curated set of internal sources by interface design, so the model cannot access unauthorized external or non-existent internal resources. Prevents hallucinations and enforces security at the tool level rather than via prompt warnings.
sr: Anthropic best-practice: ograniči sposobnost alata na validiran, kuriran skup internih izvora po DIZAJNU interfejsa, tako da model ne može pristupiti neovlašćenim eksternim ni nepostojećim internim resursima. Suzbija halucinacije i osigurava bezbednost na nivou alata, umesto upozorenjima u promptu.

en: A temporary, recoverable failure that may succeed if retried after a delay (e.g. rate limit / 429, timeout, transient 503). The correct recovery is wait-and-retry (ideally with backoff). Contrast with a terminal error, where retrying is pointless.
sr: Privremena, popravljiva greška koja može uspeti ako se ponovi posle pauze (npr. rate limit / 429, timeout, prolazni 503). Ispravna reakcija je sačekaj-pa-retry (idealno uz backoff). Suprotno od terminalne greške, gde je retry besmislen.

en: A permanent failure that will NOT succeed on retry; the input or precondition is wrong (e.g. invalid ticker / bad input, 401/403 unauthorized). The correct recovery is to change strategy — ask the user for clarification or escalate — not to retry. Contrast with a transient error.
sr: Trajna greška koja NEĆE uspeti ponavljanjem; input ili preduslov je pogrešan (npr. nevažeći ticker / loš input, 401/403 unauthorized). Ispravna reakcija je promena strategije — traži pojašnjenje korisnika ili eskaliraj — a ne retry. Suprotno od prolazne (transient) greške.

en: A boolean flag in a structured MCP error response that signals to the agent whether the error is transient and worth retrying (true -> wait-and-retry, ideally with backoff) or terminal (false -> do not retry; change strategy or escalate). The agent obeys it literally; classification accuracy is the tool's responsibility — a transient error wrongly marked false causes premature give-up.
sr: Bulev flag u strukturiranom MCP error response koji agentu govori da li je greška prolazna i vredi je ponoviti (true -> sačekaj pa probaj ponovo, idealno uz backoff) ili terminalna (false -> ne ponavljaj; promeni strategiju ili eskaliraj). Agent ga posluša bukvalno; tačnost klasifikacije je odgovornost tool-a — prolazna greška pogrešno označena kao false uzrokuje prerano odustajanje.

en: A categorical field in a structured error response that classifies the NATURE of a failure (e.g. transient / business / permission / availability), driving the agent's recovery path. Complements isRetryable (a boolean 'is it worth retrying'). It must be classified accurately — a wrong category steers the agent into the wrong strategy (e.g. an availability outage mislabeled 'permission' makes the agent ask for credentials that won't help).
sr: Kategorijsko polje u strukturiranom error response koje klasifikuje PRIRODU greške (npr. transient / business / permission / availability) i tako bira put oporavka agenta. Komplementarno sa isRetryable (boolean „vredi li ponoviti"). Mora biti tačno klasifikovano — pogrešna kategorija vodi agenta u pogrešnu strategiju (npr. availability ispad pogrešno označen kao „permission" → agent traži kredencijale koji ne pomažu).

en: jq — JSON-aware command-line processor; parses the file as structured data (objects/arrays/fields) and edits the exact field of the exact object, unlike text tools.
sr: jq — komandno-linijski procesor specijalizovan za JSON; parsira fajl kao strukturu (objekti/nizovi/polja) i menja tačno polje tačnog objekta, za razliku od tekstualnih alata.

en: Editing data through a tool that PARSES the format (jq for JSON) rather than doing literal text replacement; robust to formatting and hits exactly the targeted element.
sr: Izmena podataka preko alata koji PARSIRA format (jq za JSON), umesto doslovne tekstualne zamene; otporno na formatiranje, pogađa tačno ciljani element.

en: Safe write: the result is written to a temporary file, then the original is atomically replaced with mv; you always have either the old or the new file, never a half-written/corrupted one if the command fails.
sr: Bezbedan upis: rezultat se piše u privremeni fajl pa se original atomično prebriše komandom mv; ili stari ili novi fajl, nikad polu-upisan/korumpiran ako komanda padne.

en: Command of the Anthropic text_editor built-in tool that replaces an EXACT character string (including whitespace/newlines); literal text replacement, not structure-aware, so it breaks on formatting changes.
sr: Komanda Anthropic text_editor built-in alata koja zamenjuje TAČAN niz znakova (uklj. razmake/\n); doslovna tekstualna zamena, nije strukturno svesna — puca na promenu formatiranja.

en: Stream editor; edits text line-by-line by pattern, sees characters not structure, so it is unsafe for JSON.
sr: Stream editor; uređuje tekst red-po-red po pattern-u, vidi karaktere a ne strukturu — nesiguran za JSON.

en: Programmatic Tool Calling (PTC): a Claude capability where Claude writes code (run in a code-execution sandbox) that calls tools programmatically, instead of one model round-trip per tool call. Per Anthropic docs it is a STRONG fit for fan-out across many items, large results to filter/aggregate, and agentic search; a WEAK fit for a small number of tool calls with small responses (container/script overhead exceeds savings). Requires the code execution tool.
sr: Programmatic Tool Calling (PTC): mogućnost gde Claude napiše kôd (izvršen u code-execution sandbox-u) koji programski poziva alate, umesto jedan round-trip po pozivu. Po Anthropic docs JAK izbor za fan-out preko mnogo stavki, velike rezultate za filtriranje/agregaciju i agentic search; SLAB izbor za mali broj poziva sa malim odgovorima (overhead container-a/skripte premašuje uštedu). Zahteva code execution tool.

en: Tool boundaries: the explicit scope limits of a tool — what it does and, crucially, what it does NOT support — stated in its description so the model selects it only for in-scope cases. Part of Subdomain 2.1 (clear descriptions and boundaries). Prevents out-of-scope calls at selection time.
sr: Granice alata: eksplicitne granice opsega alata — šta radi i, ključno, šta NE podržava — navedene u opisu tako da ga model bira samo za slučajeve u opsegu. Deo Subdomaina 2.1 (jasni opisi i granice). Sprečava pozive van opsega na mestu izbora.

en: An attack where adversarial instructions embedded in user-supplied content (a chat message, a fetched document) are interpreted by the model as commands, tricking the agent into unintended actions (e.g. running cat /etc/passwd via an over-broad command tool). Robust defense removes the capability (constrain the tool interface), not just detection/filtering, which can be evaded.
sr: Napad u kome zlonamerne instrukcije ugrađene u korisnički sadržaj (poruka u chatu, preuzeti dokument) model protumači kao komande, navodeći agenta na neželjene akcije (npr. pokretanje cat /etc/passwd kroz prepirok command alat). Robustna odbrana UKLANJA sposobnost (suzi interfejs alata), ne samo detekcija/filtriranje koje se može zaobići.

en: Security principle: grant each component only the minimum capability it needs for its task and nothing more. Applied to tools, replace a generic powerful tool (execute_system_command) with a narrow, purpose-built one (check_server_ping accepting only an IP/hostname), so abuse becomes structurally impossible rather than merely policed.
sr: Bezbednosni princip: svakoj komponenti daj samo minimalnu sposobnost koja joj treba za zadatak i ništa više. Primenjeno na alate, zameni generički moćan alat (execute_system_command) uskim namenskim (check_server_ping koji prima samo IP/hostname), tako da zloupotreba postaje strukturno nemoguća, a ne samo policirana.

en: Anthropic's model splitting security duties between provider and customer: Anthropic secures the model/platform, while the customer is responsible for how they design and constrain their own tools, agents and integrations (e.g. not exposing a generic execute_system_command). Locking down tool capability is the customer's responsibility.
sr: Anthropic-ov model koji deli bezbednosne dužnosti između provajdera i klijenta: Anthropic obezbeđuje model/platformu, dok je klijent odgovoran za to kako dizajnira i ograničava sopstvene alate, agente i integracije (npr. da ne izlaže generički execute_system_command). Zaključavanje sposobnosti alata je odgovornost klijenta.

en: Claude's ordering of instruction sources by precedence: a system prompt generally outranks tool descriptions. Because of this you cannot reliably make a tool description override a system-prompt rule — fix the conflict at the higher-precedence source (the system prompt).
sr: Claude-ov redosled prednosti izvora instrukcija: sistemski prompt po pravilu ima veću težinu od opisa alata. Zbog toga ne možeš pouzdano naterati opis alata da pregazi pravilo iz sistemskog prompta — konflikt rešavaj na izvoru veće prednosti (sistemski prompt).

en: A successful operation that returns zero items (an empty list []). It is a valid outcome, NOT an error — the tool should return isError:false with the empty data and a message, so the agent distinguishes 'nothing matched' from an access/system failure. Flagging an empty result as isError:true makes the agent hallucinate a failure.
sr: Uspešna operacija koja vrati nula stavki (prazna lista []). To je validan ishod, NE greška — alat treba da vrati isError:false sa praznim podacima i porukom, da agent razlikuje 'ništa nije nađeno' od kvara pristupa/sistema. Označiti prazan rezultat kao isError:true tera agenta da halucinira kvar.

en: A tool that does exactly one operation, with a strict, purpose-specific input schema — as opposed to one overly generic/polymorphic tool that multiplexes many operations via an 'action' enum + a free-form parameters blob. Splitting a generic tool into single-purpose tools (create/update/delete) gives the model strong structural constraints, so it cannot supply parameters irrelevant to the chosen action (Subdomain 2.1). Mirrors the AWS Cloud Control API's distinct create/update/delete operations.
sr: Alat koji radi tačno jednu operaciju, sa strogom namenskom input šemom — za razliku od jednog preširokog/polimorfnog alata koji multipleksira više operacija preko 'action' enuma + slobodne parameters kese. Razbijanje generičkog alata na namenske (create/update/delete) daje modelu jake strukturne uslove, pa ne može da pošalje parametre nevažeće za izabranu akciju (Subdomain 2.1). Poklapa se sa distinktnim create/update/delete operacijama AWS Cloud Control API-ja.

en: The JSON Schema in a tool definition declaring its parameters: names, types, which are required, allowed values (enum), and whether extra fields are allowed (additionalProperties:false). A strict, narrow input_schema is a hard guardrail — a parameter not in the schema cannot be passed. Combined with strict tool use (constrained decoding), schema-invalid calls are never generated.
sr: JSON Schema u definiciji alata koja deklariše njegove parametre: imena, tipove, koji su obavezni, dozvoljene vrednosti (enum) i da li su dozvoljena dodatna polja (additionalProperties:false). Stroga, uska input_schema je hard guardrail — parametar koga nema u šemi ne može se proslediti. U kombinaciji sa strict tool use (constrained decoding), schema-nevažeći pozivi nikad ne nastaju.

en: A batch/multi-item operation outcome where some items succeed and some fail. The tool returns isError:false with the successful results PLUS a structured metadata section flagging the failures (e.g. partial_success:true, failed_items:[...]), so the agent can proceed with usable data and still see, machine-readably, what failed. Use for independent items; contrast with atomic all-or-nothing.
sr: Ishod batch/višestavke operacije gde neke stavke uspeju a neke padnu. Alat vraća isError:false sa uspešnim rezultatima PLUS strukturiranu metapodatkovnu sekciju koja označava padove (npr. partial_success:true, failed_items:[...]), pa agent može da nastavi sa upotrebljivim podacima i da i dalje mašinski čitljivo vidi šta je palo. Koristi za nezavisne stavke; suprotno od atomic sve-ili-ništa.

en: All-or-nothing semantics: an operation either fully succeeds or is fully rolled back; any single failure invalidates the whole result. Correct for true transactions with interdependent steps, but WRONG for a batch of independent items (e.g. summarizing 3 separate PDFs) — there, atomicity needlessly discards usable successful results. Prefer partial success for independent items.
sr: Sve-ili-ništa semantika: operacija ili u potpunosti uspe ili se cela poništi; bilo koji pojedinačni pad poništava ceo rezultat. Ispravno za prave transakcije sa međuzavisnim koracima, ali POGREŠNO za batch nezavisnih stavki (npr. sažimanje 3 odvojena PDF-a) — tu atomičnost bespotrebno baca upotrebljive uspešne rezultate. Za nezavisne stavke koristi partial success.

en: Examining code by reading/searching it WITHOUT executing the program (e.g. Grep to find a known identifier's call sites, then Read to inspect them). Contrast with runtime/dynamic debugging (instrumenting with console.trace + Bash to run the app). For locating usages of a known symbol, static analysis via Grep+Read is faster and non-invasive — no code mutation, no execution.
sr: Pregled koda čitanjem/pretragom BEZ izvršavanja programa (npr. Grep da nađeš call-site poznatog identifikatora, pa Read da ih pregledaš). Suprotno od runtime/dinamičkog debug-a (instrumentacija sa console.trace + Bash da pokreneš app). Za pronalaženje korišćenja poznatog simbola, statička analiza preko Grep+Read je brža i neinvazivna — bez izmene koda, bez izvršavanja.
